scope_note: Practical list of common / foraging-relevant mushrooms in Russia for a mobile-app illustration set. Includes 26 previously listed mushrooms plus 4 added species: green moss bolete, bovine bolete, grey grisette, and valui.
safety_note: This JSON is for educational app illustrations, not for field identification. Eat only mushrooms identified with certainty and processed appropriately.

mushrooms.scientific_name: Boletus edulis group, including Boletus edulis and Boletus pinophilus

edibility.processing_note: Cook before eating; often dried, fried, stewed, or pickled.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Spruce, pine, birch, and mixed forests.
mushrooms.typical_season: summer to autumn, peak often late summer to autumn
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: One of the most prized edible mushrooms in Russia.
mushrooms.risk_note: Still requires confident identification; do not collect old or damaged specimens.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

edibility.processing_note: Cook thoroughly; commonly boiled, fried, dried, or pickled.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Mixed forests, usually associated with aspen.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: Very common and widely collected.
mushrooms.risk_note: Identification should be certain; some boletes can cause digestive upset if undercooked.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

edibility.processing_note: Cook before eating; often fried, stewed, or dried.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Near birch trees in birch and mixed forests.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: Very common in Russian birch forests.
mushrooms.risk_note: Cap can become soft when cooked; avoid old or wormy specimens.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

edibility.processing_note: Cook before eating; commonly fried.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Mossy coniferous and mixed forests.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: Common, popular, and widely collected.
mushrooms.risk_note: Can be confused by beginners with false chanterelles; check identification carefully.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

edibility.processing_note: Usually cooked or salted; in Russian tradition often salted.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Pine and spruce forests.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: Highly valued edible mushroom in Russia.
mushrooms.risk_note: Orange milk and conifer association help, but identification must still be certain.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

mushrooms.scientific_name: Suillus luteus and related Suillus species

edibility.processing_note: Cook before eating; slimy cap skin is often peeled.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Young pine forests and sandy soils.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: Very common and often marinated.
mushrooms.risk_note: Some people experience gastrointestinal upset, especially if poorly prepared.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

edibility.processing_note: Must be thoroughly boiled or cooked; do not eat raw or undercooked.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Clusters on stumps, dead wood, and dying trees.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: Extremely popular for pickling and marinating.
mushrooms.risk_note: Dangerous lookalikes include Galerina marginata; small brown mushrooms on wood are risky for beginners.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

edibility.processing_note: Cook before eating; caps are often fried.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Forest edges, clearings, meadows.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: Known edible mushroom.
mushrooms.risk_note: Can be confused with smaller poisonous Lepiota-like mushrooms.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

edibility.processing_note: Cook before eating; dries well.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Damp mossy coniferous forests.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: Often found in groups late in the season.
mushrooms.risk_note: Small size makes careless collection riskier; identify carefully.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

edibility.processing_note: Cook before eating; suitable for drying, frying, or stewing.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Pine and spruce forests.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: Good edible bolete and common alternative to porcini.
mushrooms.risk_note: Pores bruise blue-green; blue staining alone does not prove safety.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

edibility.processing_note: Traditionally soaked, boiled, and/or salted before eating.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Often in birch or mixed forests.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: Classic Russian mushroom for salting.
mushrooms.risk_note: Milk caps can be acrid or irritating if improperly processed.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

edibility.processing_note: Requires soaking/boiling/salting.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Birch and mixed forests.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: Common in Russian mushroom-picking tradition.
mushrooms.risk_note: Can cause gastric irritation if not processed correctly.

edibility.processing_note: Some species are edible, some acrid or inedible; identify to species level.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Very widespread in deciduous, coniferous, and mixed forests.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: Commonly encountered and sometimes collected.
mushrooms.risk_note: Beginners should not rely on cap color; can be confused with dangerous Amanita species.

edibility.processing_note: Cook before eating; also widely cultivated.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Dead or weakened hardwood, logs, stumps.
mushrooms.typical_season: autumn to mild winter, depending on region
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: Common edible wood-growing mushroom; also common in shops.
mushrooms.risk_note: Wild wood-growing mushrooms still require expert identification.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

edibility.processing_note: Some Agaricus species are edible, while others are poisonous.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Meadows, fields, lawns, forest edges.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: Familiar because cultivated champignons are common.
mushrooms.risk_note: Can be confused with death cap or yellow-staining Agaricus.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

edibility.processing_note: Must be cooked thoroughly; do not eat raw.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Spring forests, clearings, disturbed soils, forest edges.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: Important spring edible mushroom.
mushrooms.risk_note: Can be confused with false morels, which are toxic.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

edibility.processing_note: Edible only when young and completely white inside; cook before eating.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Forests, meadows, lawns.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: Common, but must be checked carefully.
mushrooms.risk_note: Immature poisonous mushrooms can resemble puffballs; never eat if interior is not uniformly white.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

edibility.processing_note: Use only young specimens before gills blacken; cook promptly.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Lawns, roadsides, disturbed ground, grassy areas.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: Common but short-lived.
mushrooms.risk_note: Avoid roadside specimens due to contamination; deteriorates quickly.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

edibility.processing_note: Not recommended as food.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Coniferous forests, wood debris, mossy forest floor.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: Important because it can be mistaken for chanterelle.
mushrooms.risk_note: Avoid using it as a substitute for true chanterelle.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

edibility.processing_note: Do not eat.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Birch, mixed, and coniferous forests.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: Historically collected in some areas, but now considered dangerous.
mushrooms.risk_note: Can cause serious poisoning; should be excluded from edible lists.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

edibility.processing_note: Do not eat; cooking does not make it safe.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Deciduous and mixed forests; may occur near oak, birch, linden, and other trees.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: One of the most dangerous poisonous mushrooms.
mushrooms.risk_note: Can be confused with russulas or champignons; delayed symptoms make poisoning especially dangerous.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

edibility.processing_note: Do not eat; cooking does not make it safe.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Damp spruce and mixed forests.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: Important deadly white mushroom to know.
mushrooms.risk_note: Avoid all unknown white-gilled mushrooms, especially with a ring and volva.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

edibility.processing_note: Do not eat; toxins are not neutralized by cooking.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Decaying logs, stumps, buried wood.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: Critical dangerous lookalike of honey fungus.
mushrooms.risk_note: Contains amatoxins; avoid small brown mushrooms on wood unless expertly identified.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

edibility.processing_note: Do not eat.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Birch, pine, spruce, and mixed forests.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: Very common and visually iconic.
mushrooms.risk_note: Toxic and psychoactive; not an edible mushroom.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

edibility.processing_note: Do not eat.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Deciduous and mixed forests.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: Important poisonous Amanita species.
mushrooms.risk_note: Can cause severe poisoning.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

edibility.processing_note: Best avoided.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Spring forests, sandy soils, clearings, disturbed ground.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: Important because it appears in morel season.
mushrooms.risk_note: Can be confused with true morels; toxic and not recommended.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

mushrooms.scientific_name: Xerocomus subtomentosus group / Boletus subtomentosus sensu lato

edibility.processing_note: Cook before eating.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Mossy deciduous and coniferous forests.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: Common bolete, but distinct from the Polish mushroom.
mushrooms.risk_note: Can be confused with other boletes; identify by velvety olive-brown cap and yellow-green pores.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

edibility.processing_note: Cook before eating.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Pine forests, often on sandy or mossy ground.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: A Suillus bolete distinct from slippery jack because it lacks a stem ring.
mushrooms.risk_note: Look for large angular pores and ringless stem.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

edibility.processing_note: Traditionally treated as conditionally edible only after correct identification and cooking.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Mixed and deciduous forests.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: Important because it resembles other Amanita; no ring but has a volva.
mushrooms.risk_note: Key illustration trait: striate cap margin, sacklike volva, and no ring.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

edibility.processing_note: Traditionally soaked/boiled and then salted or marinated; not suitable for raw use.

mushrooms.typical_habitat_russia: Deciduous and mixed forests, often with birch or oak.
mushrooms.foraging_relevance: Specific traditional Russian mushroom distinct from generic Russula.
mushrooms.risk_note: Known for an ochre-yellow slimy cap and strong odor; should not be treated as a generic edible russula.

color_associated.basis: approximate cap/hat color from the uploaded generated illustration

map_marker_palette_note: color_associated.marker_hex is an approximate map marker color chosen from the cap/hat color visible in the uploaded generated PNG illustration. For very light mushrooms, marker_outline_hex is important for visibility on map backgrounds.
